rulings.Case Title: ELAINE PERCY VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Tax Memorandum Of Costs
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, DECEMBER 5, 2013 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 1, Plaintiffs¿ motion to tax defendant University Mechanical & Engineering Contractors, Inc.¿s memorandum of costs is granted. Defendant¿s memorandum of costs is taxed $600 for deposition costs because defendant stipulates to such reduction. Defendant¿s memorandum of costs is taxed $1,243.16 for costs identified as ¿Other¿ (hospital records and Berry & Berry services) because defendant failed to demonstrate that such costs were incurred, reasonably necessary to the conduct of the litigation rather than merely convenient or beneficial to its preparation and reasonable in amount. Allowable costs total $2,985.75. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: BILLY D ROGERS VS. 3M COMPANY, FKA MINNESOTA MINING AND et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And Motion For Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, DECEMBER 5, 2013 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 2, Defendant Oscar E. Erickson Inc.¿s motion for summary adjudication of plaintiffs¿ causes of action for false representation and intentional tort and claim for punitive damages is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden. Plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether defendant is liable pursuant to their causes of action for false representation and intentional tort. Plaintiffs failed to present clear and convincing evidence creating a triable issue whether defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
